instruction:
Implement the unified document CRUD API routes for Ship. All entity types (wiki, issue, program, project, sprint, person, weekly_plan, weekly_retro) are rows in a single `documents` table differentiated by `document_type`.

Create `routes/documents.ts` with auth middleware on all routes: GET / (list documents by workspace, filter by type/parent, respect visibility: workspace docs visible to all, private docs visible to creator and admins only), POST / (create document with Zod validation for title, document_type, parent_id, properties, visibility, content as TipTap JSON, belongs_to associations array), GET /:id (single document with visibility check, load content from yjs_state via Yjs-to-JSON converter if available, fallback to content column), PATCH /:id (partial update with document_history logging for tracked fields like title/state/priority/assignee_id, timestamp updates for issue state transitions: started_at/completed_at/cancelled_at/reopened_at), DELETE /:id (soft delete via deleted_at).

Create `routes/issues.ts` with: GET / (list issues with assignee name join, belongs_to associations batch lookup), POST / (create issue with auto-incrementing ticket_number per workspace, state enum: triage/backlog/todo/in_progress/in_review/done/cancelled, priority enum: urgent/high/medium/low/none), PATCH /:id (update with state transition timestamps and history logging).

Create `routes/programs.ts`, `routes/projects.ts`, and `routes/weeks.ts` following the same pattern. Create `routes/associations.ts` for managing document_associations (link/unlink documents to programs/projects/sprints). Create `routes/search.ts` with mention search (people + documents, trigram LIKE with SQL wildcard escape) and learning search.

Extract shared utilities to `utils/document-crud.ts`: logDocumentChange (inserts to document_history), getTimestampUpdates (maps state changes to timestamp columns), getBelongsToAssociations (fetches associations for a document). Create `middleware/visibility.ts` with VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL helper and isWorkspaceAdmin check.

Verify: create a document via POST, retrieve it via GET, update title via PATCH, confirm history entry in document_history, search for it via /api/search/mentions, soft-delete via DELETE, confirm GET returns 404.

steps.description: Confirm the existing shared visibility middleware and shared document CRUD utility satisfy the requested contracts, then extend them only where gaps remain for unified CRUD behavior. Because visibility.ts is a hub file imported by more than 8 files, preserve its exported symbols and update it compatibly rather than redesigning its API.

Implement or align the unified generic documents CRUD route so it fully matches the requested behavior: authenticated list/create/get/update/delete over the unified documents table, visibility-aware reads, Zod validation, Yjs-to-JSON fallback on single-document reads, belongs_to association handling, document_history logging for tracked fields, issue state timestamp updates, and soft delete via deleted_at.

Implement or align the issues route with the required unified-document issue behavior: visibility-aware listing with assignee name join, batch belongs_to lookup, authenticated creation with per-workspace auto-incrementing ticket_number and validated state/priority enums, and authenticated patch with document_history logging plus started/completed/cancelled/reopened timestamp handling.

Align the programs route to the same unified CRUD pattern requested by the user, ensuring it operates purely on documents rows with document_type='program', uses authenticated/visibility-aware access, and matches the shared association and update behavior expected for the unified document model.

Align the projects route to the same unified CRUD/document-association pattern, keeping project rows in documents with document_type='project', reusing visibility rules, and ensuring updates stay consistent with shared history/association utilities where relevant.

Align the weeks route to the same unified CRUD/document-association pattern for sprint/week documents, keeping week rows in documents with document_type='sprint', preserving current specialized week behavior while ensuring the requested unified route expectations and shared utilities are applied consistently.

Implement or align the associations route so it cleanly manages document_associations link/unlink operations for programs, projects, sprints, and parent relationships under auth and visibility checks, matching the user-requested dedicated associations management surface.

Implement or align the search route to satisfy both requested search surfaces: mention search over people plus documents with escaped SQL wildcard handling, and learning search over learning wiki content while preserving visibility filtering for unified documents.

Wire any route export or app-level registration changes needed so the implemented unified CRUD/search/association routes are mounted exactly where the API expects them, without changing unrelated route registrations.

fileEdits.old_string:
export async function getBelongsToAssociations(
  documentId: string,
  visibility?: AssociationVisibilityScope | VisibilityJoinScope,
): Promise<BelongsToEntry[]> {
  const query = visibility
    ? `SELECT da.related_id as id, da.relationship_type as type,
              d.title, d.properties->>'color' as color
       FROM document_associations da
       JOIN documents d
         ON da.related_id = d.id
        AND d.workspace_id = $2
        AND ${VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL('d', '$3', '$4')}
       WHERE da.document_id = $1
       ORDER BY da.relationship_type, da.created_at`
    : `SELECT da.related_id as id, da.relationship_type as type,
              d.title, d.properties->>'color' as color
       FROM document_associations da
       LEFT JOIN documents d ON da.related_id = d.id
       WHERE da.document_id = $1
       ORDER BY da.relationship_type, da.created_at`;
fileEdits.new_string:
export async function getBelongsToAssociations(
  documentId: string,
  visibility?: AssociationVisibilityScope | VisibilityJoinScope,
): Promise<BelongsToEntry[]> {
  const query = visibility
    ? `SELECT da.related_id as id, da.relationship_type as type,
              d.title, d.properties->>'color' as color
       FROM document_associations da
       JOIN documents d
         ON da.related_id = d.id
        AND d.workspace_id = $2
        AND d.deleted_at IS NULL
        AND ${VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL('d', '$3', '$4')}
       WHERE da.document_id = $1
       ORDER BY da.relationship_type, da.created_at`
    : `SELECT da.related_id as id, da.relationship_type as type,
              d.title, d.properties->>'color' as color
       FROM document_associations da
       LEFT JOIN documents d ON da.related_id = d.id
       WHERE da.document_id = $1
       ORDER BY da.relationship_type, da.created_at`;

messages.content:
Set up the PostgreSQL database layer for Ship. Create a `db/` directory under the API package with: `client.ts` (pg Pool with connection pooling, idle timeout, statement timeout, graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT), `schema.sql` (the full schema), and a `migrations/` folder for incremental changes.

Important path constraint: the canonical deliverable must live under `api/db/`, not only under the legacy `api/src/db/` path that may already exist in the repo. If existing runtime imports still point at `api/src/db/*`, convert those files into thin wrappers or re-exports only after the real implementation exists under `api/db/*`. When converting a legacy file into a wrapper, replace the entire file contents so only the wrapper/re-export remains; do not prepend exports onto the old implementation body. Do not treat editing only `api/src/db/*` as completion.

The schema must implement Ship's "everything is a document" model. Core tables: `workspaces`, `users` (with email, password_hash, x509_subject_dn, is_super_admin), `workspace_memberships` (role: admin|member), `sessions` (TEXT primary key from crypto.randomBytes, with last_activity and ip_address), `documents` (unified table with document_type ENUM for wiki/issue/program/project/sprint/person/weekly_plan/weekly_retro/standup/weekly_review, JSONB properties, JSONB content for TipTap, BYTEA yjs_state, parent_id self-reference with circular-reference prevention trigger, visibility private|workspace, soft-delete via deleted_at, ticket_number auto-increment per workspace), `document_associations` (junction table with relationship_type ENUM: parent/project/sprint/program), `document_history`, `document_snapshots`, `audit_logs`, `comments` (threaded with parent_id), `files`, `document_links` (for backlinks), `api_tokens`, `workspace_invites`, `oauth_state`, `sprint_iterations`, and `issue_iterations`. Add pg_trgm extension and comprehensive indexes (GIN on properties, trigram on titles, partial indexes on archived_at/deleted_at).

Write a `seed.ts` that creates a demo workspace, admin user (password hashed with bcryptjs), and sample documents. Write a `migrate.ts` that applies SQL files from `migrations/` in order. The initial canonical migration file must be `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql`. Verify by running `pnpm db:migrate` and `pnpm db:seed` against a local PostgreSQL instance, then querying each table to confirm it exists with correct columns. Before declaring completion, confirm that `api/db/client.ts`, `api/db/migrate.ts`, `api/db/seed.ts`, `api/db/schema.sql`, and `api/db/migrations/001_initial_schema.sql` all exist.

Implement the unified document CRUD API routes for Ship. All entity types (wiki, issue, program, project, sprint, person, weekly_plan, weekly_retro) are rows in a single `documents` table differentiated by `document_type`.

Create `routes/documents.ts` with auth middleware on all routes: GET / (list documents by workspace, filter by type/parent, respect visibility: workspace docs visible to all, private docs visible to creator and admins only), POST / (create document with Zod validation for title, document_type, parent_id, properties, visibility, content as TipTap JSON, belongs_to associations array), GET /:id (single document with visibility check, load content from yjs_state via Yjs-to-JSON converter if available, fallback to content column), PATCH /:id (partial update with document_history logging for tracked fields like title/state/priority/assignee_id, timestamp updates for issue state transitions: started_at/completed_at/cancelled_at/reopened_at), DELETE /:id (soft delete via deleted_at).

Create `routes/issues.ts` with: GET / (list issues with assignee name join, belongs_to associations batch lookup), POST / (create issue with auto-incrementing ticket_number per workspace, state enum: triage/backlog/todo/in_progress/in_review/done/cancelled, priority enum: urgent/high/medium/low/none), PATCH /:id (update with state transition timestamps and history logging).

Create `routes/programs.ts`, `routes/projects.ts`, and `routes/weeks.ts` following the same pattern. Create `routes/associations.ts` for managing document_associations (link/unlink documents to programs/projects/sprints). Create `routes/search.ts` with mention search (people + documents, trigram LIKE with SQL wildcard escape) and learning search.

Extract shared utilities to `utils/document-crud.ts`: logDocumentChange (inserts to document_history), getTimestampUpdates (maps state changes to timestamp columns), getBelongsToAssociations (fetches associations for a document). Create `middleware/visibility.ts` with VISIBILITY_FILTER_SQL helper and isWorkspaceAdmin check.

Verify: create a document via POST, retrieve it via GET, update title via PATCH, confirm history entry in document_history, search for it via /api/search/mentions, soft-delete via DELETE, confirm GET returns 404.
